title: Tapping for Working-Mum Guilt
subtitle: A five-minute tapping practice for the guilt of working while mothering
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice for the guilt that sits between working and mothering. Use it when being at work feels like abandoning them, and being present with them feels like falling behind at work.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), in use since the mid-1990s.

practice.whenToUse: Use when the guilt of working is sitting on your chest at the school drop-off, or when you get home and can't be present because the work guilt has followed you.

content.text: A five-minute tapping practice for the working-mum guilt that sits between two places. Use it in the car after the school drop-off, before a difficult meeting, or when you got home later than planned.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop (repeat x3)
Even though I feel guilty for being at work when they need me, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I cannot be in both places, and that fact makes me feel like I'm failing in both, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I cannot resolve the pull between these two things with willpower alone, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
**Eyebrow:** I feel guilty for working.
**Side of eye:** I feel guilty for not being there.
**Under eye:** The guilt follows me between places.
**Under nose:** I can't get it right in either place when I feel this way.
**Chin:** I am tired of carrying both guilts at once.
**Collarbone:** I am trying to do my best in both places.
**Under arm:** I wish I could make it feel cleaner.
**Top of head:** I'm setting the guilt down for this hour.
Reframe to positive
**Eyebrow:** Working is part of how I love them.
**Side of eye:** I am here, now, doing this work.
**Under eye:** They are cared for. So am I.
**Under nose:** I can be present in one place at a time.
**Chin:** The guilt is not evidence that I'm failing.
**Collarbone:** I am doing the thing that needs doing right now.
**Under arm:** Both parts of my life are allowed to exist.
**Top of head:** I am a good mother and I am doing good work.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from
Original to homemade.education. The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), developed in the mid-1990s from Roger Callahan's Thought Field Therapy.
